mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer confidence and discretionary spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through higher corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2017 payroll level 146,615K indicates sustained hiring momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150K.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.5% year-over-year, the Fed is in restrictive real rate territory that suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as rate expectations rise further.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2017 FEDFUNDS at 1.15% and CPIAUCSL at 244.048 index
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with subdued inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,100K units signals weakening residential investment demand that foreshadows broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerating above 0.5% month-over-month signals strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved profit margins and operational efficiency.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of months with MoM INDPRO growth > 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both declining simultaneously signals synchronized demand destruction across consumer and residential sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession risk premia increase and equity multiples compress.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2017 RSAFS at 478,873M and June 2017 HOUST at 1,215K
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction or post-shock demand collapse
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 48% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings are revised downward.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation) turns positive and remains above 0.5% for 2+ consecutive months, monetary policy is contractionary in real terms, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2017 real rate environment with FEDFUNDS 1.15% and inflation subdued
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with real rate normalization
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 52% of triggered instances, or the real rate interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are raised and risk premia compress.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 62% of quarters with annualized GDPC1 growth > 2.5%.
